ddls:
CREATE TABLE "network_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric record */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metric was recorded */,
  "latency" REAL /* Network latency at the time of recording */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost associated with the network operation */,
  "reliability" INTEGER /* Reliability score of the network */,
  "ping_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the last ping was performed */,
  "ping_time" REAL /* Time taken for the last ping */,
  "ping_success" INTEGER /* Success status of the last ping (1 for success, 0 for failure) */,
  "time_since_last_recording" REAL /* Time since the last metric recording */,
  "time_since_last_ping" REAL /* Time since the last ping */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network node */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographical region of the network node */,
  "network_id" TEXT /* ID of the network segment or subnet */,
  "vendor" TEXT /* Hardware vendor of the network node */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the network node */,
  "software_version" TEXT /* Version of the software running on the node */,
  "metric_source" TEXT /* Source of the metric (e.g., SNMP, API, manual input) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "daily_metrics" (
  "daily_metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each daily metric record */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the real-time metric */,
  "day1" INTEGER /* Aggregate metric value for day 1 */,
  "day2" INTEGER /* Aggregate metric value for day 2 */,
  "day3" INTEGER /* Aggregate metric value for day 3 */,
  "day4" INTEGER /* Aggregate metric value for day 4 */,
  "day5" INTEGER /* Aggregate metric value for day 5 */,
  "day6" INTEGER /* Aggregate metric value for day 6 */,
  "day7" INTEGER /* Aggregate metric value for day 7 */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of metric (e.g., latency, cost, reliability) */,
  "aggregation_interval" TEXT /* Time interval for aggregation (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "network_id" TEXT /* ID of the network segment or subnet */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographical region of the aggregated metrics */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("daily_metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_daily_metrics_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES network_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predicted_metrics" (
  "predicted_metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each predicted metric record */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the real-time metric */,
  "predicted_latency" REAL /* Predicted latency based on historical data */,
  "predicted_cost" REAL /* Predicted cost based on historical data */,
  "predicted_reliability" INTEGER /* Predicted reliability based on historical data */,
  "prediction_interval" TEXT /* Time interval for the prediction (e.g., next hour, next day, next week) */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the prediction */,
  "algorithm_used" TEXT /* Specific machine learning algorithm used for the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("predicted_metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predicted_metrics_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES network_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "nodes" (
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network node */,
  "node_name" TEXT /* Name of the network node */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the network node */,
  "network_id" TEXT /* ID of the network segment or subnet */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographical region of the node */,
  "vendor" TEXT /* Hardware vendor of the node */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the node */,
  "software_version" TEXT /* Version of the software running on the node */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance activity */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Scheduled date for the next maintenance activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "last_activity_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last activity */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the metric */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the metric was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user's device */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser or client */,
  "access_location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the user */,
  "access_duration" REAL /* Duration of the user's access session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES network_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network node */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date when the maintenance activity was performed */,
  "activity_description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance activity */,
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* ID of the technician who performed the maintenance */,
  "technician_name" TEXT /* Name of the technician who performed the maintenance */,
  "maintenance_duration" REAL /* Duration of the maintenance activity */,
  "maintenance_cost" REAL /* Cost associated with the maintenance activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_node_id FOREIGN KEY ("node_id") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high latency, high cost) */,
  "alert_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was triggered */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "alert_status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., open, acknowledged, resolved) */,
  "acknowledged_by" TEXT /* User who acknowledged the alert */,
  "acknowledged_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was acknowledged */,
  "resolved_by" TEXT /* User who resolved the alert */,
  "resolved_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES network_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the report */,
  "report_status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., draft, finalized, shared) */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV, HTML) */,
  "report_size" REAL /* Size of the report file */,
  "shared_with" TEXT /* Users or groups with whom the report was shared */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "queries" (
  "query_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each query */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the query */,
  "query_date" TEXT /* Date when the query was performed */,
  "query_string" TEXT /* String representation of the query */,
  "query_status" TEXT /* Status of the query (e.g., pending, completed, failed) */,
  "query_duration" REAL /* Duration of the query execution */,
  "query_result_size" REAL /* Size of the query result */,
  "query_result_format" TEXT /* Format of the query result (e.g., JSON, CSV) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("query_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_queries_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the setting */,
  "setting_category" TEXT /* Category of the setting (e.g., network, alert, user) */,
  "last_updated_by" TEXT /* User who last updated the setting */,
  "last_updated_date" TEXT /* Date when the setting was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)
